title: Local Secret Profiles are plaintext prompt material, not secure references

evidence.quote: export interface ManagedSecretEntry { id: string; key: string; value: string; }
evidence.note: Raw values are part of the ordinary Desktop settings contract.
Selected values become assistant-only prompt text.
The same message is traced, handed to prompt hooks, and durably persisted.
Assistant-audience content is retained for the provider, while trace/hook concatenation includes every text block.

observed: Saved secret values live in settings.json and renderer state, are concatenated into an assistant-audience user message, are persisted in sessions.db, are recorded into tracing fields, are sent to prompt hooks, and remain visible to the model provider.
expected_boundary: Renderer-facing credential profiles contain metadata only; raw values remain in Gosling secure storage and are resolved by a narrow backend operation without entering settings, messages, prompts, logs, traces, hooks, URLs, or renderer responses.
failure_mechanism: The feature models credentials as ordinary application data and implements credential use by prompt concatenation instead of secure backend reference resolution.
break_it_angle: Save a sentinel credential, select it for a chat, submit a message, and search settings, sessions.db, trace/hook payloads, and the provider request for the sentinel.
impact: Third-party, VPS, API, or administrative credentials can escape to local plaintext stores, hooks, logs, and model providers; compromise can affect external systems and be irreversible.
adjacent: external backend bearer secret is also an ordinary settings value
assistant-only annotations hide content from the user but expose it to the provider
user-prompt hooks receive the concatenated secret text

mitigation.minimal_repair: Remove raw values from renderer and settings contracts, migrate them to profile-specific identifiers in Gosling secure storage, and resolve references only in the backend operation that requires them.
mitigation.local_guardrail: Reject any credential-profile response or persisted record containing a raw secret value and redact assistant context before tracing or hooks.
mitigation.behavior_test: A sentinel secret is absent from settings, renderer responses/state snapshots, serialized messages, sessions.db, exports, traces, hook payloads, and provider prompt text.

implementation_assessment.rationale: The repair spans a secure-storage migration, IPC and React contracts, chat construction, observability, backend resolution, and compatibility behavior.

validation: sentinel non-disclosure tests across every persistence and IPC boundary
provider/hook/trace capture test proving no raw value is emitted
non_goals: replace Gosling's existing secure secret-storage abstraction
pre-populate stored secret values in the renderer
review_limits: The deterministic source dataflow is confirmed; which optional trace and hook sinks are enabled in a particular installation was not observed at runtime.

confidence_calibration.rationale: Raw value persistence, prompt concatenation, message persistence, trace recording, hook handoff, and provider audience filtering are explicit deterministic code paths.

skill_escalation.reason: Secret values cross settings, renderer, message, tracing, hook, and provider output boundaries.
AOC-003 prompt leakage is a direct consequence of the credential transport design.
An audience annotation is incorrectly treated as a confidentiality boundary.

title: Renderer code can self-authorize an arbitrary filesystem root

evidence.note: Every recent directory becomes an approved filesystem root.
The renderer-provided path needs only to be an existing non-symlink directory.
The same renderer can request the root and use read/write/delete/list IPC.

observed: A renderer can add any existing directory, including a filesystem or sensitive user root, to recent-dirs.json and immediately gain descendant read/write/delete/list authority through the canonical containment helper.
expected_boundary: Only main-process chooser results or backend-validated workspace grants can create scoped per-window filesystem capabilities; recent history and settings remain non-authoritative data.
failure_mechanism: The mutable recent-directory list and archive preference are used as an authorization root set, allowing an untrusted renderer to mint the authority checked by the sink.
break_it_angle: Call addRecentDir for an existing sensitive directory and then invoke readFile, writeFile, or deleteFile on a descendant.
impact: A renderer compromise can expand into arbitrary local filesystem access despite Node isolation and an otherwise correct canonical-root check.
adjacent: archiveFolder is another renderer-mutable approved root
recent-directory persistence is also non-atomic
context isolation reduces but does not remove renderer compromise risk

mitigation.minimal_repair: Separate history from authority and issue opaque per-window directory grants only from an actual chooser result or backend-validated workspace.
mitigation.local_guardrail: Direct addRecentDir and set-setting calls must never alter the root set used by file IPC.
mitigation.behavior_test: A renderer-added existing directory remains denied until the main process issues a chooser/workspace grant, and the grant expires with its window/session.

implementation_assessment.rationale: The existing containment helper can remain, but root issuance must change across chooser, preload, window lifecycle, workspaces, and file handlers.

validation: preload/main IPC contract tests for ungranted and granted paths
review_limits: No renderer exploit or destructive filesystem operation was executed; the authority-bypass composition is deterministic in source.

confidence_calibration.rationale: The same renderer has explicit APIs to mutate the authoritative root list and consume privileged file operations.

skill_escalation.reason: The renderer-to-main seam drops the policy distinction between requested path data and an issued capability.
An alternate root-enrollment path bypasses the chooser-based safety assumption.

title: Workspace folder access is metadata-only and is not propagated into tool policy

evidence.note: The public workspace model represents an enforceable-looking access distinction.

evidence.quote: Reference read-only folders without modifying them.
evidence.note: Folder access reaches the agent as prompt guidance, not as a prepared permission field.
Session creation stores the snapshot but does not set additional working roots or restrictions.
The inspector knows only session working directories and is off unless the session flag is enabled.

observed: Workspace folder access and output-root metadata is saved in the workspace snapshot and prompt, but not translated into Session.additional_working_dirs or a per-directory read/write authorization policy.
expected_boundary: New sessions pin a canonical folder policy whose read-only and read-write roots are enforced by local tools and represented consistently to MCP roots; resume preserves that policy.
failure_mechanism: The domain decision terminates at the prompt/snapshot seam instead of being propagated into session and permission state.
break_it_angle: Mark a valuable reference folder Read and request a write; separately enable directory restriction with an external output folder and observe that it is absent from allowed roots.
impact: Read-only reference data can be mutated and legitimate output/reference paths can be missing or rejected by consumers that rely on session working roots.
adjacent: working-directory restriction defaults to false
external providers may execute tools outside Gosling
Workspace UI displays a read/read-write designation that the backend does not enforce

mitigation.minimal_repair: Add a canonical per-session folder-policy snapshot and translate workspace roots into read versus mutation permissions during new-session creation and resume.
mitigation.local_guardrail: Deny mutation in read-only roots and fail visibly when a provider or tool adapter cannot honor the pinned policy.
mitigation.behavior_test: Reads succeed and writes fail for read-only roots; configured outputs accept writes; symlink traversal fails; workspace switching does not mutate an existing session policy.

implementation_assessment.rationale: The change spans SDK types, session migration, ACP create/resume, permission inspection, MCP roots, and Desktop session presentation.

validation: session creation and resume policy-pinning tests
Desktop workspace warning and session-header tests
non_goals: rely on prompt text as the enforcement mechanism
silently claim enforcement for an incapable external provider
review_limits: No live tool call was executed; the absence of folder-policy propagation and the prompt-only access instruction are confirmed statically.

confidence_calibration.rationale: The prepared session, persistence update, Session model, and only working-directory inspector have no WorkspaceFolderAccess propagation path.

skill_escalation.reason: IAPI-015 applies because a policy decision is not propagated across the workspace-to-session seam.
The UI exposes an access designation that does not match backend enforcement.
The agent and its MCP clients receive a different authority model from the workspace role/context.

title: Delegated roles inherit the parent's complete extension surface by default

evidence.quote: description": "Extensions to enable. Omit to inherit all, empty array for none."
evidence.note: Agent files cannot declare an enforceable capability policy and omission means full inheritance.
Narrowing occurs only when model-supplied params.extensions exists.
Delegated sessions always run unattended in Auto mode as SubAgent.

observed: Reviewer, scout, or other prose role intent does not restrict authority; omitted model arguments give the child every extension enabled in the parent session.
expected_boundary: Delegated role capabilities are explicit, deterministic, least-authority, intersected with parent authority, and enforced independently of model arguments and repo-authored prose.
failure_mechanism: Full capability inheritance is the default state transition and narrowing is an optional model decision; agent metadata has no enforceable allowlist.
break_it_angle: Create a repo-local reviewer agent that requests a mutation, delegate without extensions, and inspect the child's resolved extension surface.
impact: A compromised prompt or role file can turn an apparently advisory child into an unattended actor with parent mutation, network, extension, or delegation capability.
adjacent: fail-closed inspectors deny unresolved approvals but do not reduce initial authority
repo-local .gosling, .agents, and .claude role files are discovered
known-open ORCH-002 / AOC-019 design decision

mitigation.minimal_repair: Add a versioned role capability policy and make legacy/inherit-all behavior an explicit user choice instead of the omission default.
mitigation.local_guardrail: The resolved child set must be the intersection of parent capability, role policy, and explicit user/model request; built-in advisory roles default to no mutation tools.
mitigation.behavior_test: Role-by-adapter-by-mode fake-agent tests prove an advisory role cannot mutate or widen scope and that unknown roles do not silently inherit all.

implementation_assessment.rationale: Compatibility with existing agent files and the meaning of inherit-all require a product/security decision before protocol and test implementation.

validation: delegation cancellation and fail-closed inspector regressions
non_goals: allow a child to gain an extension absent from the parent
review_limits: No live delegated tool execution was performed; constructed authority is explicit in the current source.

confidence_calibration.rationale: The schema, tool description, default extension construction, and Auto child creation deterministically establish the mismatch.

skill_escalation.reason: The parent-to-child transition widens effective role authority by default.
Safety depends on an optional model field rather than an enforced default.

title: Foreign transcript import silently drops malformed JSONL records

evidence.note: Invalid lines are discarded unless every line is invalid.
The Codex converter repeats the same behavior.
Pi validates its header but silently drops malformed body records.

observed: A valid-corrupt-valid transcript imports the surviving records with no skipped count, partial marker, or qualified success.
expected_boundary: Strict import rejects every malformed nonblank record, or an explicitly chosen lenient mode reports exact omissions and persists partial provenance.
failure_mechanism: filter_map(Result::ok) conflates malformed data with irrelevant or absent data.
break_it_angle: Insert a malformed JSON line between two valid messages and import the transcript.
impact: Users can resume an incomplete history while believing all messages, tool calls, and outputs were preserved.
adjacent: AUD-GOS-006 untrusted import provenance
AUD-GOS-009 unbounded import size
partial output presented complete

mitigation.minimal_repair: Use a shared line parser that reports line-numbered errors and require explicit consent for a partial import.
mitigation.local_guardrail: Never emit an unqualified import success when any nonblank record failed to parse.
mitigation.behavior_test: A valid-corrupt-valid fixture fails strictly or returns a visible skipped=1 partial result.

implementation_assessment.rationale: Three sibling converters can share one parsing result and feed the existing CLI/Desktop import status surfaces.

validation: corrupt-middle-line fixtures for Claude Code, Codex, and Pi
Desktop and CLI import message tests
non_goals: reject intentionally ignored but syntactically valid vendor event types
review_limits: Converters were not executed; the silent discard behavior is a deterministic static property.

confidence_calibration.rationale: Each converter explicitly discards parse errors and no omission signal is returned.

skill_escalation.reason: The operator-facing import result presents partial persistence as complete success.
Round-trip completeness and provenance are lost.

title: Imported sessions promote untrusted working directories and history to authority

evidence.note: A transcript header controls the resulting working directory when nonempty.
The foreign path and conversation cross directly into canonical session creation and persistence.

observed: Syntactically valid transcript-controlled cwd and history become a normal resumable session without a durable imported/untrusted provenance state or a user-selected effective directory.
expected_boundary: Import validates and previews the canonical path, requires relinking/confirmation, and persists provenance that prevents foreign history from masquerading as locally authorized state.
failure_mechanism: Format conversion is treated as trust conversion; parse success promotes foreign data directly to session authority.
break_it_angle: Import a session whose cwd points to a sensitive local directory and whose history claims prior authorization, then resume it.
impact: A crafted import can mis-scope later file/process operations and provide prompt-injection leverage over a resumed agent.
adjacent: AUD-GOS-005 silent record loss
AUD-GOS-009 import resource bound
foreign message provenance is not modeled

mitigation.minimal_repair: Add an import review step with a user-selected effective directory and persist an imported-untrusted provenance marker on the session and messages.
mitigation.local_guardrail: Do not activate tools for a transcript-provided working directory until it is canonicalized, available, and explicitly accepted.
mitigation.behavior_test: A crafted cwd never becomes active without confirmation, and imported history remains visibly/protocol-level untrusted on resume.

implementation_assessment.rationale: The repair crosses converter output, session persistence, ACP resume, and Desktop/CLI review UX.

validation: session provenance round-trip and resume tests
non_goals: discard foreign history the user intentionally imports
silently replace cwd with the current process directory
review_limits: The authority transition is confirmed; later tool impact requires the user to resume and the agent to act.

confidence_calibration.rationale: The converter-derived field is passed directly to canonical session creation and the conversation is persisted without a provenance field.

skill_escalation.reason: Untrusted imported fields become operational paths and context.
Foreign candidate data becomes canonical active session state without a trust gate.

title: The settings IPC erases every value contract except language

evidence.quote: setSetting: async <K extends SettingKey>(key: K, value: Settings[K])
evidence.note: The renderer advertises a type-safe generic contract.
Main receives unknown, validates only the key and language, then persists any other shape.

observed: Any allowlisted setting except language accepts a wrong primitive, malformed nested object, oversized collection, or attacker-shaped value and persists it as canonical settings state.
expected_boundary: Per-key runtime schemas at the main-process IPC seam preserve the declared shape, bounds, and semantic constraints and return typed validation errors without changing disk.
failure_mechanism: Compile-time TypeScript generics are erased at IPC and no equivalent runtime validation is re-established in the privileged process.
break_it_angle: Send theme=42, keyboardShortcuts=oops, a malformed external backend object, or an oversized managedSecretProfiles value through set-setting and trigger/restart its consumer.
impact: Durable settings corruption, startup/runtime failures, and attacker-controlled values in filesystem-root and backend-trust consumers.
adjacent: AUD-GOS-002 archiveFolder authority
AUD-GOS-008 non-atomic settings write
nested object and collection size limits are absent

mitigation.minimal_repair: Create a discriminated validator map for every SettingKey in main and reject invalid values before mutating the cached object or disk.
mitigation.local_guardrail: Return a typed validation error and leave the previous record byte-for-byte unchanged.
mitigation.behavior_test: Every key has valid, wrong-type, oversized, and prototype-shaped IPC fixtures; only valid fixtures persist.

implementation_assessment.rationale: A central main-process validator map and table-driven tests can pin the existing contract without redesigning storage.

validation: table-driven runtime validation for every SettingKey
invalid input leaves settings file unchanged
non_goals: rely on renderer-side validation as the security boundary
review_limits: Wrong-typed settings were not injected at runtime; the unvalidated assignment is deterministic.

confidence_calibration.rationale: The main handler explicitly accepts unknown and validates only key membership and one key's value.

skill_escalation.reason: Invalid input becomes canonical durable settings state.
Malformed and unbounded renderer payloads cross into persistence and privileged consumers.

title: Desktop settings writes can tear and silently reset the whole profile

evidence.note: The canonical file is overwritten directly without temp, flush, rename, or restrictive mode.

evidence.quote: console.error('Failed to read settings.json, using defaults:', err);
evidence.note: Any parse/read failure is classified as a default profile.
The related recent-directory authority record repeats the same persistence pattern.

observed: Settings and recent-directory records are truncated/replaced directly; a malformed or interrupted record is logged and treated as empty/default on the next read.
expected_boundary: Owner-only unique-temp write, file flush, atomic rename, directory sync, schema validation, previous-good/quarantine recovery, and a visible recovery message.
failure_mechanism: The durable record is destroyed before its replacement is known durable, and the recovery path conflates corruption with absence.
break_it_angle: Terminate the main process or inject an I/O fault during write, then relaunch and inspect whether the old profile is recovered or silently defaulted.
impact: A single interruption can lose workflow-wide Desktop preferences, backend connection/trust configuration, archive state, and recent roots.
adjacent: AUD-GOS-001 stores high-value values in this fragile file
AUD-GOS-007 permits invalid values into the same canonical record
recent directories are both preferences and current authority roots

mitigation.minimal_repair: Use one proven owner-only atomic JSON helper for settings and recent dirs, validate before rename, and recover/quarantine a malformed current file from a previous-good copy.
mitigation.local_guardrail: Never replace the current record until the complete validated temp is durable; never silently classify corruption as a first run.
mitigation.behavior_test: Fault injection at open/write/flush/rename leaves either old or new valid JSON and produces a visible recovery state.

implementation_assessment.rationale: The repository already contains atomic persistence patterns; the Desktop needs a shared helper and failure-path tests.

validation: fault-injection tests for every atomic-write phase
non_goals: treat console logging alone as operator recovery
review_limits: No kill or disk-fault drill was run; the non-atomic replacement and default fallback are confirmed static properties.

confidence_calibration.rationale: Direct canonical-file replacement lacks every atomic-write phase and the parse-error branch deterministically returns defaults.

skill_escalation.reason: Crash recovery has neither atomic replacement nor previous-good rollback.
CON-013 applies to the non-atomic whole-file output.
A partial durable record becomes a silent all-default state.

failure_analysis.item_or_workflow: Desktop settings persistence and startup recovery
failure_analysis.failure_mode: Interrupted replacement leaves malformed JSON and startup resets to defaults
failure_analysis.likely_cause: Direct writeFileSync to the canonical record with no atomic replace or previous-good recovery
failure_analysis.local_effect: settings.json or recent-dirs.json is partial or empty
failure_analysis.workflow_effect: Desktop relaunches with lost preferences and connection/archive state
failure_analysis.system_or_operator_effect: Operator must rediscover and reconstruct configuration from memory
failure_analysis.compensating_provision: Delete the corrupt file and re-enter settings; this does not recover the lost record

single_point_failure.redundancy_or_fallback: Defaults preserve application startup but not prior user state

title: Session import materializes unbounded local and remote payloads

evidence.note: CLI materializes the complete caller-selected file.
The Electron main process returns the complete file contents over IPC.
Remote event content is decrypted to a complete String without a local cap.

observed: All import entrypoints allocate and parse the complete payload without an application-level byte budget.
expected_boundary: A shared documented maximum is enforced before materialization for local files and before/while remote fetch and decrypt, with consistent CLI/Desktop errors.
failure_mechanism: Caller- or relay-controlled payload size drives whole-buffer allocation in the CLI or Electron main process.
break_it_angle: Select a multi-gigabyte JSONL file or fetch an oversized Nostr share event.
impact: Memory pressure, responsiveness loss, or OOM on the local application process.
adjacent: AUD-GOS-005 malformed-line handling
AUD-GOS-006 imported path and provenance
remote relay content is untrusted

mitigation.minimal_repair: Define one import byte limit and enforce it before file read, IPC transfer, remote decrypt, and JSON conversion.
mitigation.local_guardrail: Reject oversize input with an actionable error before allocating the complete payload.
mitigation.behavior_test: An input one byte over the cap fails consistently in CLI, Desktop, and Nostr paths without persistence.

implementation_assessment.rationale: A shared constant and bounded readers can cover the three existing entrypoints with focused tests.

validation: memory-allocation observation for a boundary-size fixture
non_goals: introduce a streaming JSON parser in the minimal repair
review_limits: Resource exhaustion was not reproduced; only the absence of an application-level bound is confirmed.

confidence_calibration.rationale: All three complete-buffer reads are explicit and no preceding size check exists on their paths.

skill_escalation.reason: REL-005 and REL-013 apply to process memory exhaustion from unbounded work.
The remote relay path is an untrusted resource-exhaustion input.

title: Invalid server host configuration panics during startup

evidence.note: Fallible operator configuration is asserted after a constructor that otherwise returns typed errors.
The panicking parse is reached on the server startup path.

observed: An invalid GOSLING_HOST, including a hostname where numeric SocketAddr parsing is expected, reaches expect and terminates startup via panic.
expected_boundary: Configuration construction validates or parses the bind address and returns an actionable typed error without panic/backtrace semantics.
failure_mechanism: A fallible operator-input parse is encoded as an invariant after configuration deserialization.
break_it_angle: Start gosling-server with GOSLING_HOST=not-an-address or localhost.
impact: Local or service startup outage with poor recovery guidance.
adjacent: panic hook prints a forced backtrace
host is represented as String despite SocketAddr-only consumption

mitigation.minimal_repair: Return Result<SocketAddr> from socket_addr or deserialize host as IpAddr and surface the invalid environment variable through ConfigError.
mitigation.local_guardrail: No operator-controlled configuration parse on startup may call expect or unwrap.
mitigation.behavior_test: Invalid host and hostname cases exit with a typed message; valid IPv4 and IPv6 bind addresses still start parsing successfully.

implementation_assessment.rationale: The repair changes one method signature and two callers plus focused configuration tests.

validation: configuration unit tests for invalid, IPv4, and IPv6 host values
gosling-server formatting, tests, and Clippy when explicitly authorized
non_goals: add DNS resolution unless hostnames are intentionally supported
review_limits: The invalid configuration was not launched; parse plus expect is a deterministic panic path.

confidence_calibration.rationale: SocketAddr::parse returns Err for invalid/non-numeric input and the code unconditionally calls expect.

skill_escalation.reason: A fragile default-config assumption converts an operator error into a startup panic.
